assertions.reason: The browser surface could not prove the exact three-role JWT model or mutation-level requireRole() enforcement. Only researcher-level self-service access was reachable.
The self-service researcher account showed no Settings or Diagnostics navigation, and direct route entry to /settings rendered an access-denied page instead of admin controls.
No browser-visible path produced an actual reviewer session. A self-service account named reviewer still showed researcher-level controls such as Create Study.
No browser-visible path produced an actual admin session. A self-service account named admin still hit the admin diagnostics access-denied page.
Researcher denial at /settings was confirmed, but the admin-render half of the assertion could not be exercised because no browser-visible admin claims path exists.

The real browser surface provides no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; self-service password signups named reviewer/admin still landed with researcher-level UI, so role-specific behavior could not be exercised from user testing.

The real browser surface provides no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; self-service password signups named reviewer/admin still landed with researcher-level UI, so role-specific behavior could not be exercised from user testing.

blockers.description: The real browser surface provides no visible way to obtain reviewer/admin JWT role claims; self-service password signups named reviewer/admin still landed with researcher-level UI, so role-specific behavior could not be exercised from user testing.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Created self-service accounts with researcher/reviewer/admin-themed emails; the researcher and reviewer attempts both exposed Create Study, while the admin attempt still hit admin diagnostics access denied.

summary: Validated researcher-side RBAC UI restrictions, but reviewer/admin browser validation was blocked because self-service signup never produced non-researcher permissions.
